title: Configuring an On-Premises Redundant RMS License Manager Pool on Windows
breadcrumbs: Configuring an On-Premises Redundant RMS License Manager Pool on Windows
text: Configuring an On-Premises Redundant RMS License Manager Pool on Windows To configure a Redundant RMS License Manager pool with an on-premises served network license, you need local Administrator access to all machines you are using as a Sentinel license server to install the RMS License Manager and to obtain the locking criteria of each machine. The utilities you need are provided in the Tools directory of your RMS installation. Note: All RMS servers in your redundant RMS pool need fixed IP addresses. Dynamic IP addressing cannot be used for RMS servers running in a redundant setup. To set up your redundant RMS License Manager pool on Windows: Step 1: Install the RMS License Manager on each machine Download the RMS installation package from Rocket Community: Downloads onto each machine where you want to run a redundant RMS License Manager. Extract and install the package. During installation, choose the option Install RMS and I will configure the license later . Alternatively, you can extract the installation package and run Sentinel RMS License Manager.msi directly. You can find it inside the RMS/LicenseManager directory. If you use this method, the tools required for configuration are not installed. You will need to unpack and copy these manually. Do not start the RMS License Managers at this stage of the process. If you are configuring an existing RMS License Manager into a redundant RMS, stop the Sentinel RMS License Manager service. For more detailed instructions on installing the RMS License Manager, see Installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Windows . Step 2: Activate and download your License File In the course of license activation, you will need to run one of the following tools (located in the Tools directory of the RMS installation) to generate a locking code for each RMS License Manager in the pool: Wechoid (Windows interface) echoid (command line utility) Log in to the Sentinel EMS customer portal with your Entitlement ID and click Activate . The activation screen shows the number of redundant RMS License Managers defined in your Entitlement, and the Primary criteria for each machine, in this example, Disk ID . Take note of these Primary criteria as you will need them in the next steps as your locking criteria. For each machine where you have installed the RMS License Manager, enter the locking criteria (Primary criteria), and generate a locking data code using the Wechoid or echoid tool. Copy this code into the Primary n info field on the portal. To obtain the Locking Data using Wechoid: Open the Wechoid utility on the machine you want to run the RMS License Manager. Tick the Locking Criteria in the Wechoid dialog that match the Primary criteria listed on the Sentinel EMS customer portal's activation screen. For this example, it is Disk ID . Remove the check marks for any criteria that are not applicable. Generating the wrong Locking Data renders your license invalid. Copy the generated Locking Data from the Code field, including the * , and paste it into the Primary n info field on the portal. To obtain the Locking Code using echoid: Open echoid.dat from the /Tools directory. Edit the last line of the file to match the locking criteria listed on the Sentinel EMS customer portal's activation screen, and save the file. In case of multiple locking criteria, calculate the locking data by adding the hexadecimal values of the different criteria. For this example, enter 0x004 to select Disk ID as the locking criteria: # Some Examples # All criteria 0x3D9F or 11110110011111 # IP Address only 0000000000010 or 0x2 0x004 Copy Run echoid.exe from the command line. This reads the locking data from echoid.dat and generates a Locking Code. C:/Program Files (x86)/Common Files/Thales/Tools>echoid.exe Sentinel RMS Development Kit 9.8.0.0048 Host Locking Code Information Utility Copyright (C) 2021 Thales Group All Rights Reserved. Locking Code 1 : 4-*1AB CDEF HIJ KLMN Copy Copy the generated Locking Code from * to the end and paste it into the Primary n info field on the Sentinel EMS customer portal. When you have completed the previous step for all RMS License Manager hosts, click Activate . Download the generated lservrc license file and make a note of the saved location. Step 3: Configure your RMS License Manager Pool To configure your redundant RMS License Manager pool, run one of the following utilities: WRlfTool (Windows interface) rlftool (command line utility) To configure your RMS License Manager pool using the WRlfTool: Run the WRlfTool, located in the /Tools directory in your RMS installation. Click File > New . Click Add Server and add the Host Name (machine name) and the IP Address for each machine you are using as a redundant RMS. Note: The order in which you list the servers determines the order of the redundant RMS server pool. The first server listed is the leader RMS in the pool and acts as the primary RMS. The next one is the first fallback, and so forth. Click Add License . If you select to add a license file, enter the full path to the saved license file. If you choose to use the license code, enter each line of the license file individually. Note: The license code can be found inside the license file. Each line in the license file is an individual license code for a particular license feature. Copy and paste each license feature code you want to use individually into the WRlfTool. We recommend using the license file unless you wish to add only a few license features to your RMS. Click File > Save or choose File > Save As... to save the file in a different location. Close the WRlfTool. If you chose File > Save in the previous step, a folder named Thales is automatically created in the Windows Documents folder, and contains the redundant RMS license file, lservrlf . Copy the lservrlf license file into the WinNT folder of the RMS installation on each machine in your redundant RMS License Manager pool. For example, C:/Program Files (x86)/Common Files/Sentinel RMS License Manager/WinNT/ . Start the Sentinel RMS License Manager service on each machine. To configure your RMS License Manager pool using the rlftool command line utility: Run the rlftool located in the /Tools directory of your RMS installation. Create a new file for the license using the following command: rlftool -l " file name " . Enter the full path and name of the file, or point to an existing file. For example: rlftool -l "C:/Users/Desktop/lservrlf" Copy Note: You can choose your own file path and name. Take note of the location and name to refer to later. Enter Y when prompted to create a new file. This loads the file with a number of menu options. C:/Program Files (x86)/Common Files/Thales/Tools> rlftool -l "C:/Users/Desktop/lservrlf" Sentinel RMS Development Kit 9.8.0.0048 REDUNDANT LICENSE FILE TOOL Copyright (C) 2021 Thales Group All Rights Reserved. File C:/Users/Desktop/lservrlf does not exist. Create New (Y/N) or Quit(Q): y File [C:/Users/Desktop/lservrlf] loaded. [RLFTOOL MAIN MENU] 1. OPEN Open or Create New Redundant License File. 2. ADD/DELETE SERVER Add or Delete Server Names in pool. 3. POOL NAME Edit pool name. 4. PREFERENCE ORDER Change or View Preference Order. 5. SEQUENCE NUMBER Change file sequence number. 6. TIME STAMP Change file timestamps. 7. ADD LICENSE Add new license. 8. VIEW LICENSE View Licenses. 9. DELETE LICENSE Delete license. 10. CONFLICTS List or Resolve license Distribution conflicts. 11. REPORTS Generate Licenses reports. 12. EXIT AND SAVE Terminate tool. Select Option (1..12): Copy Enter 2 for the option to add or delete a server. Following the prompts, enter 1 to add a new server. Enter the server name. Enter the IP address of the server. Repeat this process for each server you are adding. Note: The order you list the servers determines the order of the redundant RMS server pool. The first server listed is the leader RMS in the pool and acts as the primary RMS. The next one is the first fallback, and so forth. To change this order, enter 4 from the main menu. From the main menu, enter 3 to view and change the pool name. Enter 7 to add the license. Enter 1 to add the license file or 2 to add the license code. If you select the license file, enter the full path to the saved license file. If you choose to use the license code, enter each line of the license file individually. Note: Each line in the license file is a license code for a particular feature. We recommend using the license file unless you wish to add only a few license features to your RMS. Enter 12 to exit and save your changes. Copy the lservrlf license file into the WinNT folder of the RMS installation on each machine in your redundant RMS License Manager pool. For example, C:/Program Files (x86)/Common Files/Sentinel RMS License Manager/WinNT/ . Start the Sentinel RMS License Manager service on each machine. Step 4: Configure your Uniface application In your application's assignment file, add the hostname or IP address of each redundant RMS to your LM_LICENSE_FILE options, separated by a semi-colon. For example: $license_options=LM_LICENSE_FILE=machinename1;machinename2;machinename3 Copy Note: The order you list each RMS must match the order of the redundant RMS pool you configured in the Redundant License Manager Configuration file ( lservrlf ). If you change the order using rlftool or WRlftool, you need to change the order in the assignment file too. Start your Uniface application. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
